subject: Fwd: Standish/Newman
body:

Darren K. Indyke

From: CMi3540012@aol.com
Subject: Fwd: Standish/Newman
Date: Mon, 18 Feb 2008 21:05:35 GMT
To: DKIESQ@aol.com, john_amerling@earthlink.net
Cc: CMi3540012@aol.com

Darren,
 
Are you comfortable with this?
 
I am not!
 
I do not appreciate being placed in this situation where we do not  have the 
documentation protecting the client in place and being asked to approve  work 
commencing.
 
We have waited months to get this started waiting a few more days  dose not 
seem unreasonable.
 
John says my call but I will defer to you if you believe it  important enough 
to wave the proper documentation.
 
Charles



**************Ideas to please picky eaters. Watch video on AOL Living.      
(http://living.aol.com/video/how-to-please-your-picky-eater/rachel-campos-duffy/
2050827?NCID=aolcmp00300000002598)


From: "John Amerling" <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
Subject: RE: Standish/Newman
Date: Mon, 18 Feb 2008 18:38:12 GMT
To: CMi3540012@aol.com, DKIESQ@aol.com

Newman will be on site tomorrow. No we do not have a contract yet, but we do
have rates. Do you want us to stop him from mobilizing? We know the scope,
we know what the GMP is. The schedule is being prepared. Our choice is to
act quickly, get to work or wait out an approval process. My vote is to act
now. Your call. Please let me know as soon as possible so that I can tell
Dan and he can let JE know.  

 

From: CMi3540012@aol.com [mailto:CMi3540012@aol.com] 
Sent: Monday, February 18, 2008 2:05 PM
To: john_amerling@earthlink.net; DKIESQ@aol.com
Cc: CMi3540012@aol.com
Subject: Fwd: Standish/Newman

 

 

 

 

 

John,

 

On Thursday the 14 th I acknowledged your Email and added a request for
schedule.

 

On the 15 th you communicated Newman was starting on Tuesday the 19 th.

 

I resent my Email because you gave the impression Newman was starting on the
19 th prior to an opportunity to review and approve budget, scope or
schedule.

 

Glad to hear some good news that we may have the ability to batch concrete
of required compressive strength.

 

Charles

 

 





  _____

From: "John Amerling" <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
Subject: Standish/Newman
Date: Fri, 15 Feb 2008 19:31:49 GMT
To: "'Darren'" <dkiesq@aol.com>
Cc: "'Charles Miller'" <cmi3540012@aol.com>

Standish says he will meet with Bill and me at 2:30 tomorrow. Newman is
sending me more info this afternoon. I will be exchanging documents and
notes with him on Monday and I will copy you in on that process before it is
consummated. It appears that we can use most of what is on the island
already, including the container. He will have a crew start work on Tuesday
morning. Monday is a holiday. All we will have lost in time is yesterday and
today in making this transition.  Again, we will use the Nash contract for
t&M work with a GMP. The deal will be detailed out to you in the contract
documents which we are working on and which will be passed by you first.
Unless something unusual happens in the interim I will be in the office on
Monday even though it is a holiday. 

 

Lafayette Contractors, LLC

6100 Red Hook Quarters, Suite A2-7

St. Thomas, VI 00802

340 779-8054 (O)

340 779-8055 (FAX)

340 690-7194 (Cell)

From:John Amerling <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
Subject:Standish/Newman
Date:Fri, 15 Feb 2008 19:31:49 GMT
To:'Darren' <dkiesq@aol.com>
Cc:'Charles Miller' <cmi3540012@aol.com>

Standish says he will meet with Bill and me at 2:30 tomorrow. Newman is sending me more info this afternoon. I will be exchanging documents and notes with him on Monday and I will copy you in on that process before it is consummated. It appears that we can use most of what is on the island already, including the container. He will have a crew start work on Tuesday morning. Monday is a holiday. All we will have lost in time is yesterday and today in making this transition.  Again, we will use the Nash contract for t&M work with a GMP. The deal will be detailed out to you in the contract documents which we are working on and which will be passed by you first. Unless something unusual happens in the interim I will be in the office on Monday even though it is a holiday.
